mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.79% remains near historical lows, signaling an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premiums, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into duration.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises by more than 15 basis points over the 20-day window despite FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.5% signals labor market tightness approaching full employment. This triggers expectations of wage acceleration and inflation, causing the Fed to signal future rate hikes. Market anticipation of tighter monetary policy drives 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 10 basis points despite UNRATE remaining below 4.7%, or forward returns are negative in > 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 470.8 billion dollars and housing starts at 1,288 thousand units indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity. Strong demand growth raises inflation expectations and real economic growth forecasts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher compensation for duration risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,200 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 12 basis points despite both RSAFS and HOUST remaining elevated, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 103.73 (index 2017=100) reflects near-trend manufacturing activity. A sustained increase in INDPRO signals supply-side capacity utilization rising, which historically precedes inflation acceleration. Market expectations of higher inflation drive 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days despite INDPRO growth exceeding 0.8%, or signal produces negative Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 145.858 million persons shows continued job growth. When combined with unemployment at 4.5%, this signals a tightening labor market where wage pressures are building. Accelerating wage growth raises inflation expectations, causing the market to price in higher terminal rates and pushing 10-year Treasury yields higher over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with accelerating employment
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 15 basis points despite both PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE conditions being met, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 243.752 (index 1982-1984=100) reflects year-over-year inflation near 2.5%. When inflation remains above the Fed's implicit target and the funds rate is at 0.79%, a widening real rate gap signals future monetary tightening. Market expectations of rate hikes drive 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance shifts.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 8 basis points over 5 trading days despite inflation-rate gap widening, or signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 16,813.3 billion chained 2017 dollars (as of Q4 2016) combined with current retail sales momentum at 470.8 billion indicates sustained economic expansion. Strong demand growth in a low-rate environment raises inflation expectations and reduces the appeal of long-duration bonds, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.4% AND GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days despite both demand indicators remaining strong, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 103.73 combined with tight labor market (UNRATE 4.5%) signals supply-side constraints emerging. When production growth slows relative to demand growth, inflation pressures build. Market expectations of stagflationary dynamics cause 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand higher real yields over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply constraint with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines by more than 12 basis points despite supply-demand imbalance signal triggering, or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.
